description: Baseline live LLM eval harness on current prompt; score already decent but typed eval script needs fixes before any improvement can be kept.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a live evaluation harness for prompt quality before tuning prompt.txt.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks failed because the new eval script had ty warnings/errors around Optional expected values.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix prompt_eval.py typing issues, rerun baseline, then start prompt experiments.
asi.baseline_findings: Current prompt misses some title cleanup and everyone-expansion cases but already handles many direct expense phrasings.

description: Add a live LLM prompt-eval harness and autoresearch scripts to measure exact extraction success before tuning prompt.txt.

asi.hypothesis: A dedicated live eval loop is needed so prompt changes can be judged on exact expense extraction and tool-readiness, not anecdotes.
asi.baseline_findings: Current prompt already handles many direct phrasings but misses some title cleanup and everyone-expansion cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect failing cases from autoresearch_eval.py verbose output, then tune prompt.txt with stronger normalization rules and few-shot examples.

description: Rewrite prompt with richer few-shot examples and add 429 retry handling, but unescaped braces in prompt.txt broke string formatting.

asi.hypothesis: More explicit extraction rules plus few-shot examples should improve title cleanup and everyone-expansion while retries shield benchmark runs from rate limits.
asi.rollback_reason: prompt.txt used literal JSON braces without escaping, so PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format raised KeyError before any eval case ran.
asi.next_action_hint: Escape all literal braces in prompt.txt, rerun the experiment, then inspect whether the new prompt improves pass_rate.
asi.error_details: Crash happened at PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format in parsing.py with KeyError '"title"'.

description: Added richer few-shot prompt plus 429 retry handling, but the prompt regressed badly on non-expense detection and started parroting example outputs.

asi.hypothesis: A more explicit prompt with examples and retry handling would improve exact extraction while stabilizing live eval runs.
asi.rollback_reason: The new prompt overfit to examples: negative inputs were misclassified as expenses, and llm nonsense test failed.
asi.next_action_hint: Redesign prompt with stronger message delimiting and a separate classification step, keeping examples but preventing example-value leakage.
asi.observed_failure: parse_with_llm('hello how are you') returned the prior 'snacks' example instead of an error, implying the prompt structure encouraged copying.

description: Prompt rewrite with clearer expense-vs-non-expense classification and message delimiting improved eval accuracy, but LLM checks hit Groq 429s before the result could be kept.

asi.hypothesis: A shorter prompt with explicit classification and tagged final input would reduce example leakage and improve extraction accuracy.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric improved, but correctness checks failed because real LLM tests were rate-limited by Groq; keep was not allowed.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the improved prompt and add bounded 429 retry handling in parse_with_llm so checks can pass under the same workload.
asi.learned: The classification-focused prompt removed false positives and raised exact-match success from 87.5% to 93.75% on the live eval suite.

description: Tighten prompt classification with tagged input plus bounded 429 retry handling in parse_with_llm; this reached a clean 100% pass rate on the live eval suite.

asi.hypothesis: Combining a shorter, classification-first prompt with explicit message delimiting and bounded 429 retries should improve accuracy while letting LLM checks survive Groq rate limits.
asi.learned: The previous few-shot prompt failed from example leakage; the successful version kept only high-signal examples, told the model to prefer not-an-expense when unsure, and tagged the final message separately.
asi.stability_note: Retrying 429s with parsed server wait times prevented checks from failing immediately after the live eval consumed most of the token budget.
asi.next_action_hint: If more work is desired, expand autoresearch_eval.py with harder paraphrases or compare response_format against tool-calling / schema-guided extraction on a new baseline.

name: Improve LLM expense extraction reliability (broader eval)

description: Broaden the live eval suite from 16 to 22 cases to reduce benchmark overfitting and cover harder natural-language expense phrasings.

asi.hypothesis: A broader workload is needed now that the original eval suite is saturated at 100%, especially for no-payer and money-related non-expense edge cases.
asi.baseline_findings: Current prompt/retry logic stays strong on the broader suite at 95.45% exact match with zero false positives.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect the single failing case, then tune prompt/parsing only if the fix improves real extraction rather than benchmark-specific wording.
asi.workload_change: Added split-among, everyone-with-payer, only-subset-splitting, plus-sign participants, money-question non-expense, and single-participant-without-payer cases.

description: Strengthen prompt rule/example so the model does not infer payer from a lone participant mention; this fixes the broader-suite failure and restores 100% pass rate.

asi.hypothesis: Explicitly forbidding payer inference from participant-only phrases like 'for Baggie only' should remove the remaining hallucinated-payer error without hurting other cases.
asi.learned: The remaining miss on the broader suite was not a general parsing weakness; it was a prompt ambiguity around participant-only phrases. A single targeted rule plus counterexample fixed it cleanly.
asi.stability_note: The retry logic continued to absorb intermittent 429s, so the improved prompt remained check-safe under the larger eval workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe fresh unseen paraphrases outside the eval suite; if they also hold up, consider stopping or adding a second benchmark focused on CLI/tool-action decision quality rather than pure extraction.

name: Improve LLM expense extraction reliability (broader eval v2)

description: Broaden the eval suite again with an unseen 'shared by X and Y' no-payer phrase to catch payer hallucinations outside the prior benchmark.

asi.hypothesis: Even after fixing 'for X only', the model may still infer a payer from participant-only phrasings like 'shared by neo and yoga', so the benchmark should cover that pattern.
asi.baseline_findings: Current prompt stays strong overall on the 23-case suite but misses the new no-payer 'shared by' pattern.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a prompt rule/example that 'shared by' or similar sharing-only phrasing identifies participants, not payer, unless someone is explicitly said to have paid.
asi.workload_change: Added shared_by_without_payer using 'movie 28 shared by neo and yoga'.

description: Added explicit no-payer rule/example for 'shared by' phrases in the prompt, but it did not improve the broader 23-case suite.

asi.hypothesis: Making 'shared by' an explicit no-payer pattern in the prompt would remove the remaining payer hallucination.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary-metric improvement on the broader suite; the added wording/example was insufficient.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect the actual failing output again and try a structurally different fix, likely parsing-side postprocessing to clear payer when response names the same set only as participants without an explicit payer cue in the source text.
asi.observed_result: Pass rate stayed at 95.65% with identical secondary metrics, so the prompt-only tweak likely left the same case failing.
